# UC-22.28.13 · Security concept documentation changes

## Description

Demonstrates alignment with BSI IT-Grundschutz expectations for security concept documentation changes using auditable Splunk evidence.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IT-Grundschutz compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes git, wiki and sourcetypes confluence:audit, gitlab:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners. Indexes required: `index=git`, `index=wiki`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=confluence:audit`, `sourcetype=gitlab:audit`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wiki sourcetype="confluence:audit" OR index=git sourcetype="gitlab:audit" earliest=-30d
| search (object="*security concept*" OR path="*konzept*")
| stats dc(author) as authors, count by object
| where authors>5
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security concept documentation changes (IT-Grundschutz)** — Demonstrates alignment with BSI IT-Grundschutz expectations for security concept documentation changes using auditable Splunk evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wiki, git; **sourcetype**: confluence:audit, gitlab:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wiki, index=git, sourcetype="confluence:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by object** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where authors>5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the BSI compliance report and the same reporting window the security team used on paper. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).

## SPL

```spl
index=wiki sourcetype="confluence:audit" OR index=git sourcetype="gitlab:audit" earliest=-30d
| search (object="*security concept*" OR path="*konzept*")
| stats dc(author) as authors, count by object
| where authors>5
```

## Visualization

Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls). Key fields: `authors`, `object`.
